behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted names (MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE=8, KEY_BLOCK_ENTRY_TYPE_INLINE_MIN=8, EntryValue::Inline, put_inline, entry_val_size, slice_from_subslice, handle_key_match, LookupValue::Slice, README '8..255' section) are explicitly mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to implement (method names, constants, behavior) but not the exact code; the developer must still write the logic.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for presence of names and general properties (e.g., extend_from_slice OR copy_from_slice), leaving flexibility in implementation.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests run cargo check, clippy, fmt, test, doc as subprocesses that compile and execute code, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: cargo test --lib exercises actual behavior; stubs for put_inline or handle_key_match would fail the repo's existing/unit tests and compile checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each required item (constants, variant, method, function, README) is checked by at least one test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section comments: Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass with clear labels.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code change, not a structured-data producer.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names modules/files which is appropriate scope; no line numbers or exact code leaked.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: README edit is minimal (two lines) and consistent with the code changes.
